description_long: This is a large, roughly rectangular stone with a rugged, uneven surface and a muted gray coloration. The stone's faces are irregular, with some flatter planes and others more jagged, suggesting it was naturally weathered or minimally shaped. There are faint orange markings visible on the top edge, possibly remnants of paint or identification tags. The texture is coarse and granular, with visible pitting and small crevices throughout. Its substantial mass and blocky form make it suitable as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or roadways. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings.
description: A large, rugged gray stone with an irregular, blocky shape and faint orange markings on its top edge. Its coarse, pitted surface and substantial mass make it ideal as an urban barrier.
description_view_0: Tall, narrow side of the stone with a prominent vertical ridge and visible orange marking on the top edge.
description_view_1: Broad, relatively flat face with subtle surface undulations and no visible markings.
description_view_2: Opposite narrow side, similar to view 0 but with less pronounced surface features and a partial view of the orange marking.
description_view_3: Another broad face, slightly more irregular than view 1, with a small section of orange marking visible at the top left.
attribute_location: urban park boundary or roadside median
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with similar stones to form a barrier
attribute_pattern: No regular pattern; surface is irregular with natural pitting and a small area of orange paint marking on the top edge.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible pitting and minor surface erosion indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or grassy urban environments, often placed to demarcate boundaries or prevent vehicle access, sometimes partially surrounded by soil or low vegetation.
